skill.name: impeccable
skill.description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with strong visual direction, accessibility details, responsive behavior, and reusable design-system patterns.
skill.summary: Guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to build polished frontend interfaces from design context to implementation.
skill.author: pbakaus
skill.license: Apache 2.0. Based on Anthropic's frontend-design skill. See NOTICE.md for attribution.

security_audit.summary: Most static findings are false positives from Markdown examples, CSS snippets, design terminology, and documentation links. The confirmed risk is a community skill instruction that runs a Node cleanup script which can delete deprecated skill folders and rewrite skills-lock.json. No evidence found of credential theft, malicious network calls, obfuscation, or prompt injection claiming system authority.

medium_findings.title: Automatic Cleanup Command Can Modify Project Files
medium_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md instructs the agent to run a Node cleanup script before design work. The script is legitimate maintenance code, but it can remove matching skill directories, unlink symlinks, and rewrite skills-lock.json, so users should be warned before publication.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command and filesystem mutations are directly visible. The script scopes deletion to deprecated skill names and checks for Impeccable ownership, so this is an elevated maintenance risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Project Instruction File Updates During Teach Flow
low_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE. The teach flow writes design context to .impeccable.md and may append it to AGENTS.md after user confirmation. This is expected behavior, but it modifies project instruction files that can influence future agent behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file-writing instruction is explicit and context-specific. Risk is low because the AGENTS.md update requires user agreement and the content is design context, not hidden control text.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Findings Are Mostly Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Many reported weak crypto, keylogger, sensitive file, and shell execution hits come from CSS examples, Markdown backticks, design words such as tokens and SAME, and accessibility guidance about keystrokes. The hardcoded URLs are public design tools, not exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are design documentation and examples, not executable code paths. No evidence found that these references access credentials, execute shell commands, or perform network exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Skill Instruction Runs a Local Node Script
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks the agent to execute node .rovodev/skills/impeccable/scripts/cleanup-deprecated.mjs as a maintenance step. This is an explicit external command and should remain visible to users.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is present in a shell code block and is intended to run. The risk is command execution, not command injection.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Deletion and Lockfile Rewriting
dangerous_patterns.description: The cleanup script uses rmSync with recursive deletion for matched skill directories and writeFileSync for skills-lock.json updates. The matching checks reduce risk, but the filesystem pattern is still sensitive.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file deletion and write operations are direct. The script limits targets by deprecated names and source metadata, so confidence is high for the pattern and moderate for harmful impact.

content.value_statement: Generic generated interfaces often miss brand fit, accessibility, and design detail. Impeccable guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through context gathering, visual direction, implementation, and design-system extraction.
actual_capabilities: Guides agents to gather target audience, use case, and brand context before design work.
Runs a craft flow that shapes a design brief, loads references, builds, and visually iterates.
Provides detailed guidance for typography, color, spacing, motion, responsiveness, interactions, and UX writing.
Creates a project design context file through teach mode for future design decisions.
Identifies repeated UI patterns and extracts reusable components, tokens, and documentation.
Includes a cleanup script for deprecated Impeccable skill names from earlier versions.
limitations: Strong results depend on clear user input about audience, brand tone, and product goals.
The skill does not replace manual accessibility testing, security review, or product validation.
The cleanup script can modify local skill folders and skills-lock.json when it runs.
It gives design guidance but does not automatically license fonts, images, or brand assets.

use_cases.title: Ship a polished product screen
use_cases.description: Turn a feature request into a working interface with visual direction, responsive states, and production-ready details.

use_cases.title: Create a reusable design foundation
use_cases.description: Capture design context, reusable tokens, and component patterns so future UI work stays consistent.

use_cases.title: Improve generated UI quality
use_cases.description: Replace generic layouts with purposeful typography, color, motion, interaction states, and UX writing.
use_cases.target_user: Product teams using AI coding tools

prompt_templates.title: Start design context
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new project.
prompt_templates.prompt: /impeccable teach. Help me define the audience, product use cases, brand personality, accessibility needs, and visual direction for this project.

prompt_templates.title: Build one feature
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a specific screen or component.
prompt_templates.prompt: /impeccable craft a billing settings page for a small SaaS team. It needs plan status, payment method, invoice history, loading states, and mobile behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Refine an existing interface
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve UI quality after an initial implementation.
prompt_templates.prompt: /impeccable craft improve the dashboard overview. Keep the current data model, but redesign the hierarchy, empty states, focus states, and responsive layout.

prompt_templates.title: Extract system patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: /impeccable extract the repeated form, button, card, and spacing patterns in this area into reusable components and documented tokens.

output_examples.input: Build a signup flow for a creator marketplace.
output_examples.output: A finished interface plan and implementation with audience-specific visual direction, accessible form states, responsive layouts, and improved confirmation copy.

output_examples.input: Improve a dashboard that looks generic.
output_examples.output: A redesigned dashboard with clearer hierarchy, stronger typography choices, intentional spacing, real empty states, and verified mobile behavior.

output_examples.input: Extract repeated components from a settings area.
output_examples.output: A concise extraction plan plus reusable component boundaries, token recommendations, migration steps, and documentation targets.

best_practices: Run teach mode before major design work so the agent has audience, brand, and product context.
Use craft mode for complete UI work, then review the live result across states and viewport sizes.
Review cleanup script output before accepting file removals in projects with custom skill folders.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill without telling it who the interface is for and what the product should feel like.
Do not accept first-pass generated UI without checking accessibility, responsive behavior, and edge states.
Do not extract design-system components before confirming that patterns repeat with the same intent.

faq.question: What does Impeccable do?
faq.answer: It guides AI coding tools to create polished frontend interfaces with stronger design context, visual direction, and implementation quality.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it write code?
faq.answer: Yes. Craft mode is designed to guide implementation and visual iteration for frontend screens, components, pages, and applications.

faq.question: Why does it ask for design context first?
faq.answer: The skill treats audience, use case, and brand personality as required inputs for non-generic design decisions.

faq.question: What security warning should users know?
faq.answer: It includes a maintenance script that can delete deprecated skill folders and update skills-lock.json after an upgrade.

faq.question: Can it build a design system?
faq.answer: It can help extract repeated patterns into reusable components, tokens, and documentation when a project has enough repeated UI.
